arbitrary_column_audit.canonicalization: the inverse absorbs j_0 into n and returns columns j_l-j_0

endpoint_pf_counterexample.consequence: the normalized endpoint sequence is not PF_3 and hence not PF-infinity

exact.bridge_boundary: deep Schur positivity of h_k=A_k(-100)/A_0(-100) is not Schur positivity of the binomially weighted Jensen-window sequences
exact.deep_target: s_lambda(h)>0 for every m>=10 and lambda in D_m
exact.dynamic_composition: deep endpoint positivity => all-order endpoint hierarchy => all-order signed-Hankel positivity on -100<=lambda<=0
exact.endpoint_deep_equivalence: [Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every m>=10,n>=0] iff [s_lambda(h)>0 for every m>=10,lambda in D_m]
exact.rectangle_target: s_((N^m))(h)>0 for every m>=10 and N>=m-1
exact.rejected_rectangle_target: s_((N^m))(h)>0 for every m>=10 and N>=m-1 is false

primary_literature_audit.citation: M. Gasca and J. M. Pena, Total positivity and Neville elimination, Linear Algebra Appl. 165 (1992), 25-44
primary_literature_audit.does_not_supply: new positivity for any open rectangle
primary_literature_audit.finding: Positive initial minors characterize strict total positivity of each finite reversed block. This is exactly the fixed-order rectangle-to-arbitrary-shape transfer already used here.

primary_literature_audit.citation: A. Edrei, Proof of a conjecture of Schoenberg on the generating function of a totally positive sequence, Canad. J. Math. 5 (1953), 86-94
primary_literature_audit.finding: The theorem classifies full Toeplitz total positivity, whereas the endpoint specialization has a rigorously negative order-three Toeplitz minor outside the deep cone.

primary_literature_audit.citation: J. M. Pena, Positive Hankel Matrices, Eigenvalues and Total Positivity, Mathematics 13 (2025), 2278, Proposition 6
primary_literature_audit.finding: Finite Hankel column reversal produces the signature (-1)^binom(k,2) in the associated Toeplitz matrix. It supports the orientation coordinate but assumes total positivity on the Hankel side and does not prove the Xi endpoint signs.

primary_literature_audit.citation: O. Y. Kushel, Matrices with totally positive powers and their generalizations, Operators and Matrices 9 (2015), 943-964
primary_literature_audit.finding: Eventual total positivity there means total positivity of all sufficiently high matrix powers, not positivity of Toeplitz minors whose Jacobi-Trudi shapes lie beyond a moving depth boundary.

proof_boundary: This artifact proves the Jacobi-Trudi index maps, their inverse, the equivalence with the existing endpoint hierarchy, and a rigorous negative PF_3 minor outside the deep cone. The order-ten source rejects the deep endpoint hierarchy itself. This does not settle the separate Jensen-window PF bridge, Jensen hyperbolicity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: Normalize the endpoint sequence so it is a genuine complete-homogeneous specialization with h_0=1.
rows.proof_boundary: A_0(-100)>0 is rigorously enclosed away from zero.

rows.claim: Reversing the contiguous Hankel columns absorbs the signed orientation and produces a solid Toeplitz determinant.
rows.proof_boundary: One finite column reversal and one transpose; no sign hypothesis is used.

rows.claim: Every signed contiguous endpoint minor is exactly a normalized rectangular Schur value.
rows.proof_boundary: The rectangle has m parts N=n+m-1, hence N>=m-1.

rows.claim: Every consecutive-row arbitrary-column signed minor maps to one deep partition.

diagnostics.canonicalization: the inverse absorbs j_0 into n and returns columns j_l-j_0

rows.proof_boundary: Increasing column gaps are exactly the adjacent partition inequalities.

rows.claim: Every partition with m parts and smallest part at least m-1 comes from a unique canonical shift and column set with j_0=0.
rows.proof_boundary: Noncanonical representations differ only by moving j_0 into n.

rows.claim: The depth inequality is exactly the condition that the whole Jacobi-Trudi matrix avoids negative-index structural zeros.
rows.proof_boundary: This defines the term deep; it does not assert positivity.

rows.claim: With the completed lower base and fixed-order transfer, the candidate endpoint hierarchy is equivalent to positivity on the entire deep Schur cone.

source_contract.candidate_endpoint_hierarchy: Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every m>=10 and n>=0
source_contract.coefficient_enclosure: rigorous acb enclosures at lambda=-100 include A_0,...,A_3
source_contract.fixed_order_transfer: strict signed contiguous layers through m imply all consecutive-row arbitrary-column signed minors through m

rows.formula: [Q_(m,n)(-100)>0 for every m>=10,n>=0] iff [s_lambda(h)>0 for every m>=10,lambda in D_m]
rows.proof_boundary: The equivalence is exact, but the order-ten counterexample makes both universal positivity statements false.

rows.claim: Within the candidate hierarchy, only rectangular deep shapes are independent; the remaining deep shapes would follow structurally from lower rectangles.
rows.formula: s_((N^m))(h)>0 for every m>=10 and N>=m-1
rows.proof_boundary: An exact coordinate for a candidate statement later rejected at order ten.

rows.claim: The normalized endpoint sequence fails ordinary PF already at the first excluded order-three solid block.

diagnostics.consequence: the normalized endpoint sequence is not PF_3 and hence not PF-infinity

rows.proof_boundary: Exact rational interval propagation from rigorous acb coefficient enclosures.

rows.claim: The deep cone is a proper subfamily of the full Schur/PF inequality family, and an additional PF inequality already fails for the actual endpoint sequence.
rows.formula: s_(1,1,1)(h)<0 but (1,1,1) is not in D_3 because 1<2
rows.proof_boundary: This blocks Edrei/ASW as a direct endpoint theorem; the separate order-ten certificate now also rejects the deep cone itself.

rows.claim: The audited primary theorems support the coordinate and transfer or impose stronger incompatible hypotheses; none supplies the candidate rectangle signs, which are now directly false at order ten.

diagnostics.citation: M. Gasca and J. M. Pena, Total positivity and Neville elimination, Linear Algebra Appl. 165 (1992), 25-44
diagnostics.does_not_supply: new positivity for any open rectangle
diagnostics.finding: Positive initial minors characterize strict total positivity of each finite reversed block. This is exactly the fixed-order rectangle-to-arbitrary-shape transfer already used here.

diagnostics.citation: A. Edrei, Proof of a conjecture of Schoenberg on the generating function of a totally positive sequence, Canad. J. Math. 5 (1953), 86-94
diagnostics.finding: The theorem classifies full Toeplitz total positivity, whereas the endpoint specialization has a rigorously negative order-three Toeplitz minor outside the deep cone.

diagnostics.citation: J. M. Pena, Positive Hankel Matrices, Eigenvalues and Total Positivity, Mathematics 13 (2025), 2278, Proposition 6
diagnostics.finding: Finite Hankel column reversal produces the signature (-1)^binom(k,2) in the associated Toeplitz matrix. It supports the orientation coordinate but assumes total positivity on the Hankel side and does not prove the Xi endpoint signs.

diagnostics.citation: O. Y. Kushel, Matrices with totally positive powers and their generalizations, Operators and Matrices 9 (2015), 943-964
diagnostics.finding: Eventual total positivity there means total positivity of all sufficiently high matrix powers, not positivity of Toeplitz minors whose Jacobi-Trudi shapes lie beyond a moving depth boundary.

rows.proof_boundary: A bounded theorem search, not a claim that no relevant theorem exists anywhere.

rows.claim: Abstract static deep endpoint positivity would feed into the completed cooperative heat induction.

source.status: exact all-order endpoint-to-heat equivalence with a completed base through order nine and the candidate endpoint hierarchy rejected by the order-ten counterexample

rows.formula: deep endpoint positivity => all-order endpoint hierarchy => all-order signed-Hankel positivity on -100<=lambda<=0
rows.proof_boundary: The implication remains exact, but its antecedent is false for the actual endpoint sequence.

rows.claim: The first four order-ten rectangles are negative, so the proposed deep endpoint hierarchy is false.

source.status: rigorous first-open-order endpoint counterexample rejecting the all-order deep rectangular hierarchy

rows.formula: s_((N^10))(h)<0 for N=9,10,11,12; s_((N^m))(h)>0 for every m>=10 and N>=m-1 is false
rows.proof_boundary: Rejects the all-order deep-cone antecedent, not RH or Jensen hyperbolicity.

rows.claim: The binomially weighted Jensen-window problem survives, but its Xi/Phi-specific antecedent must be weaker than the rejected deep cone.
rows.formula: deep Schur positivity of h_k=A_k(-100)/A_0(-100) is not Schur positivity of the binomially weighted Jensen-window sequences
rows.proof_boundary: The endpoint specialization and every finite Jensen-window specialization are different Toeplitz sequences.

exact arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel order-four theorem at lambda zero
rigorous first-open-order endpoint counterexample rejecting the all-order deep rectangular hierarchy

status: exact normalized deep-Schur coordinate and rigorous endpoint PF separation, with the all-order rectangle hierarchy rejected at order ten
